steps.title: Pin down what 'accelerated germination' requires
steps.content: The blank must give an example of accelerated germination. Acceleration is a comparison: at the same time point, H₂S-treated seeds must reach a higher germination percentage than untreated seeds. A correct choice therefore needs (a) a single time point and (b) treated vs. untreated numbers showing treated ahead.

steps.title: Match the data to that requirement
steps.content: At 48 hours, the graph shows seeds treated with 10 µmol/L of H₂S above 70% germination while untreated seeds sit around 50%. Same time point, treated clearly ahead — that is acceleration.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '24 hours...less than 10%...168 hours...more than 90%': compares the treated group to itself at two different times. No untreated comparison, so it shows growth over time, not acceleration relative to untreated. ✗
  • Choice B — '48 hours...more than 70%...only approximately 50% of untreated': same time point, treated ahead of untreated — this is acceleration. ✓
  • Choice C — '168 hours...more than 90%...less than 70% of untreated': 168 hours is the endpoint where germination has plateaued; a higher final total shows greater overall germination, not that treated seeds got there faster. ✗
  • Choice D — '48 hours, approximately 50%...only approximately 30% of untreated': misreads the graph — the 10 µmol/L curve is well above 50% at 48 hours, and untreated is near 50%, not 30%. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
